ref: I promise every living creature that the earth and those living on it will never again be destroyed by a flood.
text: Ni mahã mikpãṅmo̱ ní mike̱nye ekpãṅ le̱ amã ši, ni nu afua le̱ ebaplenẽṅ helo fẽ doṅṅ, ni asaṅ nu afua ko ebakpãtãṅ šikpoṅ le̱ hĩe gbĩkogbĩko.
translated: We are grateful for the help you gave us, we will always remember your kindness, and we wish you continued blessings.

ref: Both the cave and the land that goes with it were bought from the Hittites.
text: Abō̱ le̱ ke̱ te̱kplo ni yo̱ mli le̱ ahe ye̱ Hetbii le̱ adeṅ.
translated: I'll go to the market and buy some food for my family.

ref: The king said to him, “I had a dream, yet no one can explain what it means. I am told that you can interpret dreams.”
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef ake̱: Mila lamõ ko, ni mokomoko be̱ ni nyẽ šiši tšõ; ni minu ni akē̱ ye̱ ohe ake̱ ke̱dši onu lamõ ko le̱, onyẽo̱ šiši otšõo̱.
translated: **I, Pharaoh Joseph, say: Let the people be, for they are the ones who have been given the land; I will give them what they need, and they will live in peace.**

ref: The king said to Joseph, “It's good that your father and brothers have arrived.
text: Ni Farao kē̱ Iosef akē̱: Otše̱ ke̱ onyemimei le̱ eba oṅõ ne̱;
translated: We are Pharaoh and Joseph: they have called us something else;

ref: Joseph and his wife had two sons before the famine began.
text: Ni Iosef fo̱ bibĩĩ enyo̱, dãni hõmo̱ afii le̱ ba, mei ní eke̱ Asenat, On oso̱fo Potifera biyō le̱, fo̱ le̱.
translated: I, Joseph, am here, and my family lives with me, we are from Asenat, and we are going to Potifera, that is all.

ref: That's why Benjamin must be with us when I go back to my father. He loves him so much
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ agbe̱ne̱ ke̱dši mite otšũlo̱, mitše̱, ṅõ ni gbekẽ le̱ fataa wo̱he le̱, ake̱ noni gbekẽ ne̱ susuma he esusuma kpe̱te̱ hewo̱ le̱,
translated: If you do not respect your elders, you will have no good; but if you respect them, you will receive blessings.

ref: When your father said the speckled sheep would be my wages, all of them were speckled. And when he said the spotted ones would be mine, all of them were spotted.
text: Ke̱dši ekē̱: Nii ni be yo̱ *truntrãi le̱ atšṍ onyõmo̱wo le̱, bĕle̱ tōi le̱ fẽ efo̱ truntrãi; ni ke̱dši ekē̱: Nii ni he yo̱ sãmãisãmãi le̱ atšṍ onyõmo̱wo le̱, bẽle̱ tōi le̱ fẽ efo̱ nii ni he yo̱ sãmãisãmãi. *
translated: If you have it good in the forest, then you will get it; if you have it hard in the forest, then you will get it.

ref: His second son was named Ephraim, which means “God has made me a success in the land where I suffered.”
text: Ni ewo moni dši enyo̱ le̱ Efraim, ni ekē̱: Edšake̱ Nyoṅmo̱ ehã miwo ſĩei ye̱ mimanehũlũ šikpoṅ le̱ no̱.
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough knowledge of the Gaa language to provide an accurate translation.

ref: with three branches. As soon as it budded, it blossomed, and its grapes became ripe.
text: ni egbla nidši etẽ, ni efĩmo̱ afofrokpo̱i, ni egba ni ewo yibii šabii ni etšu;
translated: The people are here, the strangers are far away, and the child who does not work today.

ref: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ni šikpoṅ le̱ kwẽ dſe̱i, bāi ni woo̱ wui ye̱ eheno̱, ke̱ tšei ní woo̱ yibii ní ewui le̱ yo̱ emli ye̱ eheno̱. Ni Nyoṅmo̱ na ake̱ ehī.
translated: I’m sorry, but I can’t translate that text.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Ni Adam kē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ene̱ dši wu ni dše̱ miwui le̱ amli ke̱ lō ní dše̱ mihelo le̱ mli; aatšé̱ mone̱ yō, edšake̱ nū mli adšiele̱ ke̱-dše̱.
translated: “I am Adam: I have been living here for a long time and I do not want to leave; please help me, I am tired of this life.”

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Ni ehĩe Abram dšogbāṅ ye̱ ehewo̱, ni ená tōi ke̱ tšinai ke̱ tedšihĩĩ ke̱ we-hĩĩ ke̱ weyei ke̱ tedšiyei ke̱ yomai.
translated: When Abram was old, he said that his son would inherit his possessions and his descendants would inherit his name.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Ni mibile̱ ake̱: Namo bi dšio? Ni ekē̱: Betuel, Nahor bi ni eke̱ Milka fo̱ le̱, bi dšimi. Ni miṅõ ga le̱ miwo egugõṅ, ni miṅõ ninešigai le̱ miwo enine;
translated: We are together: How are you? We are: Betuel, Nahor are my friends. Milka is here, we are happy. We are going to the market, we are preparing food for the family.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ etu enibii fẽ ní eyo̱ le̱ ewo Iosef deṅ, ni ekwe̱e̱ enokonoko ake̱ dša nii ní eyeo̱ ke̱ke̱. Ni Iosef he ye̱ fe̱o ni ehĩe hũ ye̱ fe̱o.
translated: We are grateful for the help that Joseph gave, because he always supports us and we thank him.

ref: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Iakob kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekpakpā mibii ye̱ mihe: Iosef be̱, ni Simeon be̱, ni nyebaṅõ Benyamin hũ ke̱-ya; ene̱mei ne̱ fẽ le̱ mino̱ kã!
translated: I am Jacob, my father is …: Joseph is, and Simon is, and also Benjamin is; let us go together!

ref: After taking everything of value from the houses, they dragged away the wives and children of their victims.
text: Ni ame̱ṅõ ame̱henii fẽ ke̱ ame̱bii bibii fẽ ke̱ ame̱ṅãmei nom, ni ame̱ha ame̱we nibii fẽ.
translated: If you do not understand your own language, you will be misunderstood by others.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Ni ame̱ṅõ Sodom ke̱ Gomora nii fẽ ke̱ ame̱niyenii nom, ni ame̱yiṅ ame̱te.
translated: If you have been to Sodom and Gomorrah and you have not repented, then you will be condemned.

ref: Jacob knew it was Joseph's coat and said, “It's my son's coat! Joseph has been torn to pieces and eaten by some wild animal.”
text: Ni eyo, ni ekē̱: Mibi le̱ atade le̱ ni! *kolo fõṅ ko eyele̱, tšere̱mo̱ etšere̱ Iosef mli ne̱! *K.
translated: You are here, you are fine: My friend, take care of yourself! Let us all go together, Joseph will be there!

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: omo̱bo̱nale̱i ke̱ onokwayeli fẽ ni odšie otšõ otšũlo̱ le̱, ekomeflo ne̱ke̱ *esaami; ši tšo mihĩe ke̱-fo Iordan ne̱, ni agbe̱ne̱ mitšṍ asafokui enyo. *
translated: Translation not available

ref: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
text: ke̱ Amorbii ke̱ Kanaanbii ke̱ Girgašbii ke̱ Iebusbii le̱.
translated: Amorbii, Kanaanbii, Girgašbii, Iebusbii

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Ni Adam kē̱: Agbe̱ne̱ ene̱ dši wu ni dše̱ miwui le̱ amli ke̱ lō ní dše̱ mihelo le̱ mli; aatšé̱ mone̱ yō, edšake̱ nū mli adšiele̱ ke̱-dše̱.
translated: Translation not available

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Ni ehĩe Abram dšogbāṅ ye̱ ehewo̱, ni ená tōi ke̱ tšinai ke̱ tedšihĩĩ ke̱ we-hĩĩ ke̱ weyei ke̱ tedšiyei ke̱ yomai.
translated: The text is a passage describing Abram’s actions and his relationship with the people, stating that he went to the land, performed certain deeds, and was honored among them.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Ni mibile̱ ake̱: Namo bi dšio? Ni ekē̱: Betuel, Nahor bi ni eke̱ Milka fo̱ le̱, bi dšimi. Ni miṅõ ga le̱ miwo egugõṅ, ni miṅõ ninešigai le̱ miwo enine;
translated: We are not sure: What are you doing? We are: Betuel, Nahor and I are Milka’s children, we are together. We are going to the market tomorrow, we are going to the farm tomorrow.

ref: Potiphar left everything up to Joseph, and with Joseph there, the only decision he had to make was what he wanted to eat. Joseph was well-built and handsome,
text: Nohewo̱ le̱ etu enibii fẽ ní eyo̱ le̱ ewo Iosef deṅ, ni ekwe̱e̱ enokonoko ake̱ dša nii ní eyeo̱ ke̱ke̱. Ni Iosef he ye̱ fe̱o ni ehĩe hũ ye̱ fe̱o.
translated: We thank you for the help that Joseph gave, and we also thank the elders for their support. Joseph also gave us food and gave us water.

ref: who said, “You have already taken my sons Joseph and Simeon from me. And now you want to take away Benjamin! Everything is against me.”
text: Ni ame̱tše̱ Iakob kē̱ame̱ ake̱: Nyekpakpā mibii ye̱ mihe: Iosef be̱, ni Simeon be̱, ni nyebaṅõ Benyamin hũ ke̱-ya; ene̱mei ne̱ fẽ le̱ mino̱ kã!
translated: I am Jacob, son of ...: Joseph is, Simeon is, and Benjamin is my beloved; let us go and worship!

ref: Magdiel, and Iram. These clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom, the father of the Edomites. They took their names from the places where they settled.
text: lumo̱ Magdiel, lumo̱ Iram. Mene̱mei dši Edom lume̱i le̱ ye̱ ame̱šihĩle̱hei le̱ anà ye̱ šikpoṅ ni ame̱ná le̱ no̱. Le̱ dši Esau ni dši Edombii atše̱ le̱.
translated: Hello Magdiel, hello Iram. I am Edom, the one who is called the son of Esau and the son of Edom.

ref: Both this pile of rocks and this large rock have been set up between us as a reminder. I must never go past them to attack you, and you must never come past them to attack me.
text: Ne̱ke̱ ku ne̱ dši odase, ni ne̱ke̱ okadite̱ ne̱ hũ dši odase, ake̱ mi le̱ mihoṅ ku ne̱ he mibaṅ oṅõ, ni bo hũ ohoṅ ku ne̱ ke̱ okadite̱ ne̱ he obaṅ miṅõ ye̱ efõṅ-femo̱ gbe̱.
translated: If you do not work, you will die; and if you do not eat, you will die. However, my mother says my father is alive, and if you do not work, you will not have children to inherit the land.

ref: Lot went to the men who were engaged to his daughters and said, “Hurry up and get out of here! The Lord is going to destroy this city.” But they thought he was joking, and they laughed at him.
text: Ni Lot dše kpo ni eke̱ ešãhĩmei le̱ ní baw̌e̱ ebiyei le̱ wie ekē̱: Nyetea ši *nyedšea bie̱ ne̱, ši Iehowa bakpãtã maṅ ne̱ hĩe! Ši etamo̱ moni yeo̱ fe̱o ye̱ ešāhĩmei le̱ ahĩe. *
translated: Now the Lord has spoken to his people: Let us worship Jehovah our Father who loves us! This is a message for all who hear the word.

ref: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
text: Na, midamo̱ nubu ne̱ he, ni mã le̱ṅ bii abiyei le̱ mīdše kpo mība nuyē̱:
translated: Now, I don’t know, and we will go to the market tomorrow, then we will see.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Ni Iakob ſo̱ Rahel nā, ewó egbẽ no̱ ni efó.
translated: We, Jacob and Rachel, are children of my father.

ref: Unfortunately, your mother Rachel died in Canaan after we had left northern Syria and before we reached Bethlehem. And I had to bury her along the way.
text: Ši mi le̱, beni midše̱ Mesopotamia mihaä le̱, *Rahel gbo ye̱ mihe ye̱ Kanaan šikpoṅ le̱ no̱, ye̱ gbe̱ teṅ, beni eſe̱ gbe̱ kukuō ko ni aše̱o̱ Efrat le̱, ni mifũle̱ ye̱ Efrat ni dši Betlehem le̱ gbe̱ le̱ mase̱i. *
translated: This is me; we are from Mesopotamia. Rachel, the mother of Kanaan, is with us. We go to Efrat and settle there, near Bethlehem, where we will build a house.

ref: But the Egyptian magicians used their secret powers to do the same thing. The king did just as the Lord had said—he stubbornly refused to listen.
text: Ni *Mizraim ṅkũnyāyelo̱i le̱ ke̱ ame̱ṅkũnyāyeli-ṅāi le̱ fe nakai noṅṅ. Ni awadše Farao tšui ni ebooame̱ toi, take̱ boni Iehowa ewie le̱. *K.
translated: We, the Egyptians, are the people who have been enslaved. We told Pharaoh that we will be saved, but Jehovah will punish him.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Ni ake̱ nii ba mã ehĩe ni eye; ši eke̱ ake̱: Miyeṅ nii, ake̱ dša midšadše nõ sane ní mihĩe ke̱-ba le̱ dã. Ni ekē̱: Wiemo̱!
translated: You are not here with my friend, right? But still: I am here, I’m going to work now, and I won’t see you later. You say: Welcome!

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Ni e̱etšṍ gbomo ní tamo̱ ṅãṅ tedši; enine aakã mofẽmo he, ni mofẽmo nine aakã ehe; ni eehĩ ši ye̱ enyemimei le̱ fẽ hĩe.
translated: You should not ignore the elders' counsel; those who do not respect their parents will not have peace, and you will encounter many difficulties.

ref: Judah asked his friend Hirah take a goat to the woman, so he could get back the ring and walking stick, but she wasn't there.
text: Ni Iuda ṅõ tōkpakpobi le̱ ehã enanyo Adulamnyo le̱ ke̱-te, koni eyahe ewabatōnii le ye̱ yō le̱ deṅ; ši enaa yō le̱.
translated: I’m sorry, but I can’t translate that text.

ref: Joseph said: This is the meaning of your dream. The three branches stand for three days,
text: Ni Iosef kē̱le̱ ake̱: Ešišitšõmo̱ ne̱; nidši etẽ le̱ gbĩĩ etẽ ni;
translated: Translation not available

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ni eba mli ake̱ dāne̱ ké̱ tōi ni be wa le̱ ná mũsũ le̱, Iakob ṅõo̱ tšei le̱ eſieo̱ tōi le̱ ahĩe ye̱ ahĩmai le̱ amli, koni ame̱ná mũsũ ye̱ tšei le̱ ahe.
translated: You have not yet learned that you are not listening to the story; Jacob said that the thing is here, but the story is still not over.

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Ni beni efoṅ ye̱ Peniel le̱, hũlũ dše ye̱ eno̱, ni etšutšo̱ ye̱ egboṅ le̱ mli.
translated: We are going to Peniel’s house today; we will go there; we will pray at the house.

ref: But I know that the king of Egypt won't let you go unless something forces him to.
text: Ši mi le̱ mile ake̱ Mizraim maṅtše̱ le̱ eṅmẽṅnye gbe̱ ní nyeya, dša ninešiwale̱ nā.
translated: This is my friend from Egypt, whose people are good, but they are not here.

ref: The two men met with the other leaders of their city and told them:
text: Ni Hamor ke̱ebi Šekem le̱ ba ame̱mā le̱ṅ agbó le̱ nā, ni ame̱ke̱ ame̱mã le̱ṅ hĩĩ le̱ wie ní ame̱kē̱:
translated: If Hamor says that the child will be born, then the mother will go to the farm, and if the child is born, the mother will stay at home.

ref: The governor of Egypt was rude and treated us like spies.
text: Nū le̱ ni dši šikpoṅ le̱ no̱ nuṅtšo̱ lē̱ tutu ehĩe ewowo̱, ni ebuwo̱ šikpoṅ le̱ sralo̱i.
translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough knowledge of Ga to provide an accurate translation of that text.

ref: Jacob's two grandsons who were born there made it a total of
text: Ni Iosef bii ni efo̱ ye̱ Mizraim le̱ dši susumai enyo̱. Iakob we le̱ fẽ ni ba Mizraim le̱ nā šio̱ *susumai nyoṅmai-kpawo. *
translated: And Joseph went to Egypt with his family. Jacob also went to Egypt and settled his family there.

ref: The jailer did not worry about anything, because the Lord was with Joseph and made him successful in all that he did.
text: Gbokle̱foi akwe̱lo̱ le̱ kwe̱e̱ nokonoko ni yo̱ edeṅ le̱; edšake̱ Iehowa ke̱le̱ ye̱, ni nofẽno ni efeo̱ le̱, Iehowa hãa eſereo̱.
translated: All those who have been called in this world and you are; the Lord is great, and his love and his mercy, the Lord is holy.

ref: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
text: Ni eke̱: Ba šĩa, bo moni Iehowa edšō̱o! mẽhewo̱ odamo̱ ṅmaṅmaša? Ši midientše̱ misā šĩa le̱ ke̱ gbe̱ hũ mihã yomai le̱.
translated: How are you? Come here, you praise Jehovah! Are you eating? I am going to the house now with you.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Mene̱mei dši Noa sešibii le̱: Noa dši *dšalo̱, ni eye emū ye̱ eyino̱bii le̱ ateṅ; ni Noa ke †Nyoṅmo̱ nyie. *Heb.
translated: I am unable to translate this text.
